xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

Group-wise RNA Expression Enrichment Analysis

Source: R/enrich_by_hpa.R
enrich_by_hpa.Rd

Applies enrichment testing across multiple groups (e.g. tissues, brain regions, immune cell types) using Human Protein Atlas (HPA) RNA expression data. Expression values are aggregated per gene per group and used either directly as GSEA rankings or discretised for hypergeometric testing.

enrich_by_hpa(
  gene_list,
  reference_df,
  group_col = c("Brain.region", "Subregion", "Immune.cell", "Tissue"),
  expression_col = c("pTPM", "nTPM", "TPM", "Tags.per.million",
    "Scaled.tags.per.million", "Normalized.tags.per.million"),
  method = c("hypergeometric", "gsea"),
  cutoff_type = c("threshold", "quantile", "top_k", "specificity"),
  cutoff_value = 0.9,
  universe = NULL,
  universe_type = c("global", "group", "provided"),
  min_overlap = 1L,
  min_background = 10L,
  gene_stats = NULL,
  alternative = c("greater", "less", "two.sided"),
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

gene_list

Character vector of gene symbols (the query set).

reference_df

Data frame of HPA RNA expression data. Must contain a Gene column, a grouping column, and an expression value column.

group_col

Character scalar. Column to group by. Auto-detected from c("Brain.region", "Subregion", "Immune.cell", "Tissue") if not supplied.

expression_col

Character scalar. Expression value column. Auto- detected from c("pTPM", "nTPM", "TPM", "Tags.per.million", "Scaled.tags.per.million", "Normalized.tags.per.million") if not supplied.

method

Character. "hypergeometric" (default) or "gsea".

cutoff_type

Character. How to define expressed genes for the hypergeometric test. Ignored when method = "gsea".

"threshold"

Genes with mean expression strictly above cutoff_value.

"quantile"

Genes at or above the cutoff_value quantile within the tissue (must be in (0, 1)).

"top_k"

Top cutoff_value genes by mean expression (cutoff_value must be a positive integer).

"specificity"

Genes whose mean expression in the tissue is at least cutoff_value-fold above the mean across all other tissues. Equivalent to TissueEnrich's tissue-enriched gene definition (cutoff_value = 5). Unlike the other cutoff types, this is computed from the full reference_df before the per-group test, so the data frame must contain all tissues. Produces much smaller gene sets than "quantile" and is more appropriate when you want tissue-exclusive rather than tissue-high genes.

cutoff_value

Numeric. Threshold value interpreted according to cutoff_type. Default 0.9 (suitable for "threshold" and "quantile"; set 5 for "specificity"; set an integer for "top_k").

universe

Optional character vector. Required when universe_type = "provided".

universe_type

Character. Background gene universe construction:

"global"

All unique genes in reference_df.

"group"

Only genes measured within the group being tested.

"provided"

Use universe directly.

min_overlap

Integer. Minimum overlap between gene_list and the group universe. Groups below this threshold are silently skipped. Default 1.

min_background

Integer. Minimum background size to attempt enrichment. Default 10.

gene_stats

Optional named numeric vector. Used only when method = "gsea" and universe_type = "provided".

alternative

Character. "greater" (default), "less", or "two.sided".

seed

Optional integer seed for GSEA permutations.

...

Additional named arguments passed to run_enrichment() for GSEA (n_perm, gsea_weight, adaptive, adaptive_mode, alpha, eps).

Value

A data frame with one row per group that passed filters, containing enrichment results and a padj column (BH adjustment across groups). Returns an empty data frame if no groups pass.

See also

run_enrichment(), enrich_by_hpa_ihc(), build_tissue_sets()

Contents

Developed by Artur Shvetcov.
